mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.3pp, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving sustained positive equity returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: Joint exceedance of 12-month moving averages yields negative 60-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output growth that outweighs rate hike pricing, resulting in negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day returns in >60% of observations, or the regime-conditional coefficient flips sign in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 4.0% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative short-term equity returns as discount rates spike.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 305.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates robust labor demand absorption, supporting corporate earnings expectations and positive quarterly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150k and UNRATE declines > 0.1pp simultaneously
failure_condition: The joint condition yields negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly boosts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon through fundamental valuation channels.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization gains that outpace input cost pressures, driving positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO accelerations in recovery regimes are followed by negative 5-day returns in >65% of instances, or the signal loses predictive power when tested against a buy-and-hold benchmark.
